seoTitle: Fix PayPal Friends and Family Option Missing (2026)
metaDescription: Learn why the Friends and Family payment option disappears on PayPal and how to restore it using account settings or a PayPal.Me link.
intro: If the personal Friends and Family payment option isn't showing when you try to send money, it's usually tied to your account type, region, or a hidden checkout toggle. This guide covers the common causes and how to work around them. It's aimed at users trying to send a fee-free personal payment that keeps defaulting to commercial mode.

steps.heading: Check the recipient's region and account type
steps.body: Some jurisdictions restrict personal fee-free transfers, and Business account recipients may not display the personal option at all.

steps.heading: Retry the payment through Send & Request
steps.body: Go to Send & Request and retype the recipient's verified handle to reload the checkout screen.

steps.heading: Look for Change Payment Type
steps.body: On the payment summary screen, look for a Change Payment Type link and click it to reveal the hidden option.

steps.heading: Select Sending to a Friend
steps.body: Choose the option to explicitly re-enable the personal transfer stream.

steps.heading: Try a PayPal.Me link as a backup
steps.body: If the option still won't appear, open the recipient's PayPal.Me link as an alternative direct personal routing path and complete the transfer using your balance or bank.

faq.question: Why is Friends and Family hidden for some recipients?
faq.answer: Business accounts or regional restrictions can automatically hide the personal payment tab from the checkout flow.

faq.question: Does a PayPal.Me link always show the personal option?
faq.answer: It's a reliable alternative route that often bypasses interface quirks blocking the standard checkout screen.

faq.question: Is Friends and Family always fee-free?
faq.answer: It's generally free when funded by your balance or bank account, though card-funded transfers can carry a fee.

faq.question: Could a browser issue cause this problem?
faq.answer: Yes, cookie or script conflicts can sometimes force the checkout interface into merchant mode, so clearing cache is worth trying.
